sync_policy.synchronization_rule: Keep ticket status aligned with canonical lifecycle status/phase whenever safe and possible. When canonical history is blocked by integrity drift, mark planning tickets as blocked until repair or explicit recovery unblocks new work.

tickets.title: Normalize helper success and follow-up semantics
Normalize structured lifecycle and state failure responses
Clarify and enforce lifecycle field semantics
Recover prior session context during resume across manual Zed session changes
Make helper tx.begin bootstrap canonical-aware and resume-safe
Implement canonical-aware helper tx.begin bootstrap guard
Repair canonical ticket ID collision handling and global tx_id uniqueness
Add regression tests for workflow guidance contract

status_context.reason: Canonical history is currently blocked by duplicate tx.begin caused by global ticket ID collisions: p2-t01 was reused across multiple versions, so new 0.5.4 lifecycle events collided with prior canonical transactions. Until repair is complete, downstream implementation work such as p2-t06 must remain blocked even though p2-t01 is functionally complete.
status_context.recommended_action: Start p2-t07 first to repair the ticket ID collision issue, document and enforce globally unique tx_id/ticket_id naming, and then resume blocked follow-up work such as p2-t06 only after canonical history is trustworthy again.
